children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “National Security Agency Voluntary Separation Act”.
the term “Director” means the Director of the National Security Agency; and

children.label: A
children.text: a reemployed annuitant under subchapter III of chapter 83 or chapter 84 of title 5 or another retirement system for employees of the Government; or

children.label: B
children.text: an employee having a disability on the basis of which such employee is or would be eligible for disability retirement under any of the retirement systems referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Establishment of program
children.label: c Establishment of program
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Director, in his sole discretion, may establish a program under which employees may, after , be eligible for early retirement, offered separation pay to separate from service voluntarily, or both.October 1, 2000
is at least 50 years of age and has completed 20 years of service; or
has at least 25 years of service,

children.heading: Amount of separation pay and treatment for other purposes
children.label: e Amount of separation pay and treatment for other purposes
A
children.text: an amount equal to the amount the employee would be entitled to receive under if the employee were entitled to payment under such section; orsection 5595(c) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: be a basis for payment, and shall not be included in the computation, of any other type of Government benefit; and

children.label: B
children.text: be taken into account for the purpose of determining the amount of any severance pay to which an individual may be entitled under based on any other separation.section 5595 of title 5

children.heading: Reemployment restrictions
children.label: f Reemployment restrictions
children.text: An employee who receives separation pay under such program may not be reemployed by the National Security Agency for the 12-month period beginning on the effective date of the employee’s separation. An employee who receives separation pay under this section on the basis of a separation occurring on or after , and accepts employment with the Government of the United States within 5 years after the date of the separation on which payment of the separation pay is based shall be required to repay the entire amount of the separation pay to the National Security Agency. If the employment is with an Executive agency (as defined by ), the Director of the Office of Personnel Management may, at the request of the head of the agency, waive the repayment if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position. If the employment is with an entity in the legislative branch, the head of the entity or the appointing official may waive the repayment if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position. If the employment is with the judicial branch, the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts may waive the repayment if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position.March 30, 1994section 105 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: act as agent or attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person (except the United States) in any formal or informal appearance before, or, with the intent to influence, make any oral or written communication on behalf of any other person (except the United States) to the National Security Agency; or

children.label: B
children.text: participate in any manner in the award, modification, or extension of any contract for property or services with the National Security Agency,

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: 2 Penalty
children.text: An employee who violates an agreement under this subsection shall be liable to the United States in the amount of the separation pay paid to the employee pursuant to this section multiplied by the proportion of the 12-month period during which the employee was in violation of the agreement.
with the prior approval of the Director;
for the period specified by the Director; and
to employees within such occupational groups or geographic locations, or subject to such other similar limitations or conditions, as the Director may require.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: Before an employee may be eligible for early retirement, separation pay, or both, under this section, the Director shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Notification of exercise of authority
children.label: j Notification of exercise of authority
children.text: The Director may not make an offer of early retirement, separation pay, or both, pursuant to this section until 15 days after submitting to the congressional intelligence committees a report describing the occupational groups or geographic locations, or other similar limitations or conditions, required by the Director under subsection (h), and including the proposed regulations issued pursuant to subsection (i).

children.heading: Remittance of funds
children.label: k Remittance of funds
children.text: In addition to any other payment that is required to be made under subchapter III of chapter 83 or chapter 84 of title 5, the National Security Agency shall remit to the Office of Personnel Management for deposit in the Treasury of the United States to the credit of the Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, an amount equal to 15 percent of the final basic pay of each employee to whom a voluntary separation payment has been or is to be paid under this section. The remittance required by this subsection shall be in lieu of any remittance required by section 4(a) of the Federal Workforce Restructuring Act of 1994 ( note).5 U.S.C. 8331
